skill.name: how-to-create-claude-code-skill
skill.description: A guide to creating Claude Code Skills.
skill.summary: A guide to creating Claude Code Skills.
skill.author: ClaudeCode-NPC

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged shell commands, home directory paths, hardcoded URLs, and weak cryptography patterns in SKILL.md. Review found these are documentation examples and Claude documentation links, not executable bundled code or malicious behavior. The skill is low risk because it teaches users to create Claude Code skills and includes example filesystem and command snippets.

low_findings.title: Documentation Shell Command Example
low_findings.description: Static analysis flagged a bash block that shows python scripts/helper.py input.txt. This is an instructional example, no helper script is bundled, and no automatic execution path is present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The command appears inside a guide section about referencing files and helper scripts. I found no evidence of executable skill code or user input being passed to a shell.

low_findings.title: Documentation Home Directory Path Example
low_findings.description: Static analysis flagged creation of ~/.claude/skills/my-skill-name. This is a documented installation path for personal skills, not hidden-file access by the skill itself.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The line is under a personal skills storage location heading. It does not read, write, or exfiltrate data from the home directory.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded Documentation Links
low_findings.description: Static analysis flagged hardcoded URLs. The URLs point to Claude documentation pages used as citations for skill best practices and structure guidance.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URLs are visible citation links in prose. I found no evidence that the skill performs network requests or sends data to those URLs.

low_findings.title: Markdown Backtick and Weak Crypto False Positives
low_findings.description: Static analysis flagged shell backticks and weak cryptography patterns, but the reviewed lines contain metadata, template prose, or inline field names. No shell execution or cryptographic algorithm use was found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file is a Markdown guide and contains no crypto API calls. The duplicated line 14 alert appears to be triggered by ordinary text, not a security-relevant implementation.

dangerous_patterns.title: Example Shell Command Requires User Judgment
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill includes a generic command example for running a helper script. Users should inspect helper scripts before execution, although this package does not include such a script.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The command is visible and instructional, so the risk is user-mediated. The absence of bundled executable code reduces confidence that this is an actual threat.

content.value_statement: Teams need clear structure when creating reusable Claude Code skills. This guide explains required files, optional references, storage locations, naming patterns, and practical organization.
actual_capabilities: Explains the required SKILL.md file and YAML front matter.
Shows optional reference, examples, scripts, and templates folders.
Describes personal and project skill storage locations.
Summarizes progressive disclosure for loading supporting files.
Provides naming examples and organization patterns for multiple skills.
limitations: It is a short guide, not a full interactive scaffold generator.
It does not validate a skill package automatically.
Some examples are general and may need updates for a specific environment.
It includes Chinese instructional text, which may require translation for some teams.

use_cases.title: Create a First Skill
use_cases.description: Use the guide to set up a basic SKILL.md file with a valid name and description.

use_cases.title: Plan a Larger Skill Package
use_cases.description: Use the folder examples to decide when to add references, examples, scripts, or templates.

use_cases.title: Organize Shared Skills
use_cases.description: Use the project structure examples to group related skills by workflow or domain.

prompt_templates.title: Draft a Basic Skill
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this guide to draft a simple Claude Code skill for writing release notes.
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner user creating a first skill

prompt_templates.title: Review My Skill Structure
prompt_templates.prompt: Compare my skill folder layout with this guide and identify missing required or optional files.

prompt_templates.title: Plan Supporting Files
prompt_templates.prompt: Use progressive disclosure guidance to decide which reference files, examples, scripts, or templates my skill should include.
prompt_templates.scenario: Team designing a more complex skill

prompt_templates.title: Create a Skill Library Plan
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this guide to design a project-level Claude Code skills directory for code review, documentation, and testing workflows.
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced user organizing several related skills

output_examples.input: Create a skill for testing code.
output_examples.output: A concise skill plan with a SKILL.md file, clear front matter, usage instructions, and optional examples for common test workflows.

output: A project skills directory grouped by workflow.
Optional reference files loaded only when needed.

best_practices: Keep the SKILL.md body focused and under the recommended length.
Use specific action-oriented names that describe the skill workflow.
Place detailed references in separate files and load them only when needed.
anti_patterns: Do not use vague names such as Helper, Utils, or Tools.
Do not put every detail in SKILL.md when reference files would be clearer.
Do not add executable scripts without documenting when and why they should run.

faq.question: What does this skill help me create?
faq.answer: It helps you create a Claude Code skill with a clear SKILL.md file and supporting structure.

faq.question: Does it generate files automatically?
faq.answer: No. It provides guidance and examples that an assistant or developer can follow.

faq.question: Can it help with project-level skills?
faq.answer: Yes. It shows how to place skills under a project .claude/skills directory.

faq.question: Does it include security scanning?
faq.answer: No. It is a creation guide and does not scan packages for security issues.

faq.question: What tools does it support?
faq.answer: The report marks it as compatible with Claude, Codex, and Claude Code workflows.

faq.question: Is this suitable for advanced skills?
faq.answer: Yes, but advanced users may need additional validation, tests, and detailed reference files.
